steps.text: Collect a percentage of samples for laboratory analysis to compare with results from the same location to validate the scan-only methodology.

Compare measurement uncertainty calculated from measured values for specific radionuclides from the site survey with the required method uncertainty to determine if the project MQOs for the radionuclides are met.
Calculate the standard deviation of the net count rate, or the statistical counting uncertainty, using Equation 6-17.

constraints.text: The uncertainty of a measurement expressed as combined standard uncertainty must include the counting uncertainty of the measurement instrumentation and the sum of the errors associated with the measurement system.

steps.text: Expand the subsection on the first MQO, Measurement Method Uncertainty in Section 4.8.4.1, with clear descriptions of the concepts of Measurement Method Uncertainty, Required Method Uncertainty, the calculation of the Required Method Uncertainty, and its use in the DQO process.
Include detailed, step-by-step, worked out examples of setting up σ and uMR uncertainty component lists for a few cleanup scenarios with the expected measurement systems to be used.
Provide specific, detailed examples of how to calculate the CSU for different measurements/methods.
Provide a fully worked out example of determining an MDC in Chapter 6.

constraints.text: Regardless of the difficulty of calculating or estimating uncertainty of a measurement, each measurement should have an associated uncertainty that is used in the final standard combined uncertainty.

steps.text: Remove DRPs soon after their discovery during characterization surveys.
Use the Unity Rule for dose assessment and compliance with dose limits.

preconditions.text: DRPs are discovered during characterization surveys.

constraints.text: Do not use MARSSIM guidance established for area or volumetric sources for DRPs.
Do not treat the area around a DRP as an EMC.

steps.text: Explain how one reaches the new conclusion (45 required samples versus 15 required samples) to make the example more understandable.
Better describe the usefulness of the method in the radiometric testing context.
Provide a more thorough discussion in support of examples, including method selection criteria and how to use the results for decision making.
Add alternative methods, such as regression or calibration.
Strengthen the basis for including ranked set sampling, or consider dropping Appendix E entirely.
Delete the reference to area factors in Section 5.3.8.
Reiterate the information from Section 4.5.2 (that derivation of the DCGLEMC is beyond the scope of MARSSIM and strictly determined by regulatory agencies) in Section 8.6.1.
Add the material in Sections 2.4.3-2.4.6, covering all survey types, to Section 4.3, along with Figures 2.4-2.8, or reference this material in Section 4.3.
Revise the headings in the bulleted paragraphs in Section 4.2.1 to mimic the DQO steps, as presented in the introduction to Section 4.2.
Make Revision 2 available for public comment.
Incorporate suggested improvements as appropriate based on the public comment review.

preconditions.text: Input from the SAB should be sought for any significant changes, including those currently proposed for Revision 2.

constraints.text: The MARSSIM document does not address volumetric or subsurface soils.

steps.text: Add a statement to Section 5.1, Introduction, indicating that Appendix O provides detailed calculations for statistical tests, illustrative examples for the determination of DCGLs, and more detailed derivations of key statistical concepts.
Add a statement to Section 5.3.3 indicating that additional information on the WRS Test including an example is provided in Appendix O (O.2).
Add a statement to Section 5.3.4 indicating that additional information on the Sign Test including an example is provided in Appendix O (O.3).
Add a statement to Section 5.3.5 indicating that additional information on DCLGEMC including an example is provided in Appendix O (O.4).

Add text to Section 5.3.3.2, p. 5-31, when describing Table 5-2, stating that these values were calculated using Equation O-1 in Appendix O and increased by 20% to account for missing or unusable data and uncertainty.
Change the reference in Section 5.3.5 (p. 5-36, line 3) from Section 4.2.5 to Section 4.5.2.
Modify the statements in Section 5.3.5.2, Example 8, p. 5-41 to change L to 10.7 m and the resulting grid area to 99.1 m2.
